signals.formula_text: overall = clamp( round1( Σ_i w_i * c_i * decayFactor * antiSpamDampening * breakoutMultiplier + quietKillerBonus ), 0, 100 ). Weights sum to 1.0 (re-normalized per category). c_i ∈ [0,100]. Modifier ranges: decay ∈ [0.3,1.0], antiSpam ∈ [0.3,1.0], breakoutMult ∈ [1.0,1.5], quietKillerBonus ∈ [0,10].
signals.formula_pseudocode:
base = sum(weights[k] * components[k] for k in COMPONENT_KEYS)
after = base * modifiers.decayFactor * modifiers.antiSpamDampening * modifiers.breakoutMultiplier
overall = clamp(round1(after + modifiers.quietKillerBonus), 0, 100)
inputs: starsDelta24h (int, missing→0 in ingestion defaults; 300/309 repos in repos.jsonl have 0)
contributors (int; used as denominator so growth% not contribs abs)
openIssues (int; linear 0..200 → 0..100 — larger project = higher 'activity', conflates health with bug-debt)
categoryAvgStarVelocity7d (filled by scoreBatch pre-pass; reduces to 0 when called via scoreRepo)

windows.starVelocity24h: 24h (snapshot delta from latest vs capture ≤ now-86.4M ms)

sensitivity.doubling_starsDelta24h_from_50_to_100: logNorm(50,500)=62.8; logNorm(100,500)=74.1; Δcomponent ≈ +11.3; at w=0.20 ⇒ base +2.26 points (before modifiers).
sensitivity.doubling_starsDelta24h_from_500_to_1000: logNorm(500,500)=100; logNorm(1000,500)=100 (clamped). No change — ceiling saturation.
sensitivity.mentions_to_zero: socialBuzz component (weight 0.12 default, 0.18 for AI cats) drops to 0; mentionCount24h=0 also disables breakout criterion 3 and HackerNews detector; antiSpamDampening x0.5 gate only fires at starsDelta24h>500 AND socialBuzz<10, which rarely co-occurs in practice.
sensitivity.small_to_large_forks: Δ0→50 forks/7d ⇒ logNorm(50,300)=68.2 → at w=0.08 ⇒ +5.5 base points.

signals.cold_start_behavior: A brand-new repo with 5 stars IS in the candidate set: there is no stars floor in the scoring path. Even a 0-star repo with 0 deltas still gets a score (will surface ~22 from commitFresh=95 + releaseFresh=95 + issueActivity=0 + communityHealth=50). Movement classification will usually return 'declining' or 'stable'. Breakout is GATED at stars ≥ 10 and stars < 1000 (modifiers.ts:117) — so 5 stars ⇒ never breakout. Quiet killer gated at stars ≥ 100 (modifiers.ts:185) ⇒ never fires for 5-star repo. `contributorGrowth30d` returns 0 when contributors ≤ 0 (components.ts:66).
signals.anti_spam: See dedicated signal below. Active in scoring modifiers (modifiers.ts:64-94).

signals.formula_text: fires when starsDelta24h > 20 AND dailyAvg7d > 0 AND ratio = starsDelta24h / dailyAvg7d > 2. Confidence: ratio ≥ 4 → 'high', ≥ 2.5 → 'medium', else 'low'.
signals.formula_pseudocode:
if delta24 <= 20: return null
dailyAvg7d = delta7 / 7
ratio = delta24 / dailyAvg7d
if ratio <= 2: return null

sensitivity.doubling_delta24_from_40_to_80_at_delta7: ratio 4.0x→8.0x, triggers same signal but confidence shifts low→high. Binary trigger, ratio only affects confidence label.
sensitivity.delta7=0_case: dailyAvg7d=0 ⇒ early-return null. This is the STATE OF EVERY SEED REPO except chroma-core/chroma in current .data/repos.jsonl.

signals.cold_start_behavior: Requires starsDelta7d > 0 AND starsDelta24h > 20. A new repo with <20 stars/day will never trigger this signal. No absolute-stars gate, so small repos qualify IF they pass deltas.
signals.anti_spam: None here — anti-spam lives only in the scoring modifier, not in the reason detector. A botspike of +500 stars/24h with near-zero forks fires star_velocity_up AND star_spike at high confidence with no dampening at the reason layer.

signals.formula_text: fires when stars > 50 AND starsDelta24h > 0 AND (starsDelta24h / stars) > 0.05. High confidence if pct > 0.10.
signals.formula_pseudocode:
if repo.stars <= 50: null
pct = delta24 / stars
if pct <= 0.05: null

sensitivity.worked_example: stars=1000, delta24=150 ⇒ pct 15% ⇒ high. stars=10000, delta24=150 ⇒ pct 1.5% ⇒ null.

signals.cold_start_behavior: Gated at stars > 50 — brand-new 5-star repo is excluded.
signals.anti_spam: None at detector level. Bot-spike of 1000 fake stars on a 5k-star repo registers as 20% spike = 'high' with zero scrutiny.

signals.formula_text: Breakout multiplier fires when stars ∈ [10, 1000) AND at least 2 of 3 criteria hold: (1) starsDelta24h > 3 * (starsDelta7d/7); (2) starsDelta24h/stars > 0.05; (3) socialBuzzScore > 30 AND mentionCount24h ≥ 3. Multiplier = clamp(1.1 + maxIntensity*0.4, 1.0, 1.5).
signals.formula_pseudocode:
if stars<10 or stars>=1000: not breakout
crit1 = delta24 > (delta7/7)*3
crit2 = delta24/stars > 0.05
crit3 = social>30 AND mentions24h>=3
if count([crit1,crit2,crit3] where true) < 2: not breakout
mult = 1.1 + max(intensities)*0.4, capped at 1.5
file_refs: src/lib/scoring.ts:154-190 (legacy — different: requires ALL of acceleration+social AND abs delta24≥10, single-criterion intensity formula)

sensitivity.doubling_delta24_from_15_to_30_at_stars_: Crit1: 15/(70/7)=1.5 → fail; 30/10=3.0 → still fails > strict (needs >3, not ≥3). Crit2 (delta24/stars): 15/500=3% → fail; 30/500=6% → crit2 fires. Crit3 passes always. Transitions 1-of-3 → 2-of-3, NOT breakout → breakout.
sensitivity.mentions_to_zero: Crit3 dies (mentions<3). With only crit1+crit2 possible, a pure bot-spiked small repo CAN still be breakout. Social-validation guarantee the code COMMENT claims is broken — it's only one of three criteria.

signals.cold_start_behavior: stars<10 ⇒ never breakout. A new repo with 5 stars fails the gate.
signals.anti_spam: Has structural overlap with antiSpamDampening (which fires at deltas>200), but neither references mentionCount24h robustly. Below 200 stars-spiked AND social=0, antiSpam NEVER fires (starsDelta24h>200 gate). So a 999-star repo with +150/24h and 0 forks and 0 social passes breakout Crit2 (15%) cleanly, unscrutinized.

signals.formula_text: Fires when ALL hold: stars ∈ [100, 50000]; starsDelta7d ≥ 30; starsDelta24h < starsDelta7d/3; commit freshness > 55 (i.e. commit within ~14d); contributors ≥ 5; contributorsDelta30d > 0. Bonus = clamp(consistencyFactor*5 + growthFactor*5, 0, 10).
signals.formula_pseudocode:
inBand = 100 <= stars <= 50000
weeklyOk = delta7 >= 30
noSpike = delta24 < delta7/3
active = freshness(lastCommit) > 55
teamOk = contributors >= 5
teamGrowing = contribsDelta30d > 0
if any missing: null
consistency = 1 - |delta24 - delta7/7| / (delta7/7)  # 0..1, capped
growthRate = contribsDelta30d / contributors
growthFactor = clamp(growthRate / 0.25, 0, 1)  # 25% monthly → 1.0
bonus = clamp(consistency*5 + growthFactor*5, 0, 10)
file_refs: src/lib/scoring.ts:204-239 (legacy — different: 30≤dailyAvg≤200, no contributor gate, no 50k star cap, sparkline-variance CV instead of contributor-growth factor)

sensitivity.doubling_contribsDelta30d_from_5_to_10_a: growthRate 10%→20%; growthFactor 0.4→0.8; bonus contribution +2.0 before cap.
sensitivity.no_history_delta7_lt_30: null. This is EVERY repo in current .data except chroma (which has delta7=138 but delta30d=0 and contribsDelta30d=0 so still null).

signals.cold_start_behavior: stars<100 OR contributors<5 ⇒ not quiet killer. A 5-star or <5-contributor repo is categorically excluded.
signals.anti_spam: The no-spike rule (delta24<delta7/3) is the implicit anti-spam filter for this detector — a 200-star spike in 24h on a weekly-100 baseline cannot be a quiet killer. Weak because it's still window-based, not sourced from fork/contributor/social cross-checks.

signals.formula_text: fires when previousRank - currentRank > 5. Confidence: jump ≥ 25 high, ≥ 12 medium, else low.
signals.formula_pseudocode:
if previousRank undefined: null
jump = previousRank - repo.rank
if jump <= 5: null

windows.window: between compute cycles — depends on when caller last persisted a rank

sensitivity.rank_tie_at_startup: previousRank is passed in from ReasonInput. If storage never persisted prior ranks, every repo is undefined → detector never fires. Current reasons.jsonl: 0 rank_jump hits out of 309 repos.

signals.cold_start_behavior: Requires a previousRank. Brand-new repos with no prior rank ⇒ never fire.
signals.anti_spam: None. A ghost-rank jump caused by a sibling repo leaving the list (shifting everyone up) is indistinguishable from real climbing.

signals.formula_text: release_recent: 0 ≤ daysSince(lastReleaseAt) ≤ 7. release_major: regex /(^|[^0-9])v?(\d+)\.0(\.0)?($|[^0-9])/ on lastReleaseTag AND daysSince ≤ 14.

sensitivity.non_semver_major_tag_like_langchain-core: Matches MAJOR_VERSION_RE because regex is anchored on '[^0-9]' delimiters — accepts the '1.3' partial? Actually no — regex requires N.0 literal. BUT langchain's reasons.jsonl line 2 shows 'release_major' firing on 'langchain-core==1.3.0a3' — meaning current metadata has a non-matching tag being MARKED major. Verify: '==1.3.0a3' — MAJOR_VERSION_RE = /(?:^|[^0-9])v?(\d+)\.0(?:\.0)?(?:$|[^0-9])/i hunting for '1.0' etc. Actually the substring 'a3' after 1.3.0 means the regex sees '3.0a' — the 'a' is [^0-9] so '(?:$|[^0-9])' matches. That's matching v0.0/v1.0/v3.0 in ANY substring. Fragile.
sensitivity.tag_without_release_date: days computed only if lastReleaseAt non-null — null ⇒ returns null cleanly.

signals.cold_start_behavior: No star/size gate. Even a 0-star repo that shipped v1.0 yesterday would fire release_major at high confidence.
signals.anti_spam: None. Regex matching of 'major' is substring-based and naive — easily fooled by tags like 'v0.10.0-beta' (no, that's '10.0' match), or 'release-2.0-hotfix' (matches). No check for whether a prior release existed or whether this is a genuinely new semver major.

signals.formula_text: factor = 1.0 * (0.6 if starsDelta24h>200 AND (forksDelta7d/stars)<0.005) * (0.5 if starsDelta24h>500 AND socialBuzzScore<10) * (0.4 if stars>1000 AND commitFreshness<20 AND contributors<3). Floored at 0.3.
signals.formula_pseudocode:
factor = 1.0
ratio = forksDelta7d/stars  # NOTE: uses 7d delta, not total forks
if delta24>200 AND ratio<0.005: factor *= 0.6
if delta24>500 AND social<10: factor *= 0.5
if stars>1000 AND freshness(commit)<20 AND contribs<3: factor *= 0.4
return clamp(factor, 0.3, 1.0)

sensitivity.bot_spike_100_stars_on_5star_repo: delta24=100 < 200 ⇒ gate 1 fails. No dampening applied.
sensitivity.bot_spike_300_stars_no_social_big_repo: delta24=300 & social=0 — gate 1: needs delta24>200 (yes) AND forksDelta7d/stars<0.005. On a 20k-star repo with typical +50/7d forks ratio is 0.0025 → fires. factor*=0.6. Score reduced 40%.
sensitivity.doubling_delta24: Unless it crosses 200 or 500 boundary, NO sensitivity — multiplicative heuristics are binary gates.

signals.cold_start_behavior: All three gates are effectively upper-bound gates (require large delta or large stars). A brand-new 5-star repo cannot trigger any of them; antiSpam=1.0 by default. This means 'anti-spam' does NOT protect small repos from vanity-star attacks, which is exactly the attack surface most vulnerable.
signals.anti_spam: This IS the anti-spam mechanism. Issue: comment at modifiers.ts:67-74 admits they use forksDelta7d instead of absolute fork count because ModifierInput doesn't carry absolute forks. So gate 1 misfires on fast-moving legit forks (delta7 temporarily dips), and misses spam on repos that already had high total forks. The 'forkRatio' variable is miscalibrated.

momentum_0_100_math.formula: overall = clamp( round1( (Σ w_k * c_k) * decayFactor * antiSpamDampening * breakoutMultiplier + quietKillerBonus ), 0, 100 )

normalizations.issueActivity: linearNorm(openIssues, 0, 200) — NOT issue velocity, just absolute open count
normalizations.communityHealth: fixed 50 when flags undefined (current default for all repos)
normalizations.categoryMomentum: logNorm(categoryAvgStarVelocity7d, ceiling=250); zero when scoreRepo called without categoryAverages

momentum_0_100_math.weight_resolution: resolveWeights(categoryId) merges DEFAULT_WEIGHTS + CATEGORY_WEIGHT_OVERRIDES[category] then divides by sum so weights sum to 1.0. Fallback to defaults if no category match.
momentum_0_100_math.theoretical_max: 100 * 1.0 * 1.0 * 1.5 + 10 = 160, but clamped to 100. This means a breakout-multiplier boost is often wasted (saturation).

backtest_observations: Snapshots.jsonl covers ONLY 13 distinct repos over ~1 month (2026-03-19 through 2026-04-17), 304 lines total. The other ~296 seed repos have zero history — their deltas are 0 by construction.
repos.jsonl shows 300/309 repos with starsDelta24h=0 and 309/309 with socialBuzzScore=0 and mentionCount24h=0. Only chroma-core/chroma has non-trivial deltas (24h:4, 7d:138).

scores.jsonl distribution: max overall score is 45.5 (chroma at 42.6, nextjs/tokio around 40-45). 172/309 are 'declining', 127/309 'stable', only 6 'rising' (chroma, tanstack/router, argoproj/argo-cd, semgrep, tokio, slint). 0 'hot', 0 'breakout', 0 'quiet_killer'.

reasons.jsonl: 0 breakout_detected, 0 quiet_killer_detected, 0 rank_jump, 0 star_velocity_up, 0 star_spike, 0 fork_velocity_up, 0 contributor_growth, 0 hacker_news_front_page, 0 viral_social_post, 0 social_buzz_elevated, 0 category_top. Only commit_fresh, release_recent, release_major, organic_growth fire.

The entire 'trend + breakout detection' claim is currently VAPOR in production data: the pipeline has no history, no deltas, no social, so the trend engine is running on a skeleton input and produces no signals.
The 40k-star stargazer-backfill ceiling has not been hit in data — the limitation is absence of ANY stargazer history, not the ceiling.

known_blowups_check.notes: Present in seed-repos.ts:62 but not in .data/repos.jsonl — ingestion never completed for it.
MCP repos in seed but absent from data/
Listed at seed-repos.ts:60 but not in persisted repos.jsonl.

known_blowups_check.flagged_as: stable, movement='stable', score 21.9, reasons=[release_major, release_recent, commit_fresh]
known_blowups_check.notes: Has 169k stars and +0 starsDelta24h/7d/30d in the data. No snapshot history — engine cannot see any growth. Score pinned by commitFresh=95, releaseFresh=95, issueActivity=100. No trend/breakout signal despite being a mega-repo.

known_blowups_check.flagged_as: rising, score 42.6, reasons include commit_fresh (NOT star_velocity_up despite delta7=138)
known_blowups_check.notes: starsDelta24h=4, starsDelta7d=138. dailyAvg7d=19.7. star_velocity_up requires delta24>20 — fails by 16 stars. star_spike requires delta24/stars>0.05 — gets 4/27472=0.0001, fails. Only 'rising' because overall>=35 AND delta7>50 triggered basic classifier.
133k stars, 0 deltas. 'release_major' fires on tag 'langchain-core==1.3.0a3' — that's a pre-release alpha tag being misclassified as MAJOR due to substring regex matching '3.0a3'.
Stale last-release from 2025-06-26 so releaseFresh=0 → decayFactor=0.716, penalizes score correctly in spirit.

fp_patterns: MAJOR_VERSION_RE is substring-based and flags pre-release/alpha tags like 'langchain-core==1.3.0a3' as release_major — already happening in production reasons.jsonl.
issueActivity component: linearNorm(openIssues, 0, 200) rewards abandoned bug-swamps. Any 10-year-old repo with 200 stale issues scores 100 on this component. NOT velocity-based, NOT age-normalized.

antiSpamDampening uses forksDelta7d in place of total forks (modifiers.ts:67-74 acknowledges the approximation). A legitimate repo that happened to have zero forks added this week is labeled spam; a repo with 10k old forks but sudden 500-star 24h spike with real botting passes (delta is >0).

breakout_detected requires 2-of-3 criteria. Criterion 2 alone (relDailyGrowth>5%) can be satisfied by a 20-star-to-100-star vanity spike; combined with the accel ratio being easy at low bases, 2-criterion 'breakout' is noisy for tiny repos.
categoryMomentum logNorm(categoryAvg, 250) is zero by default when scoreRepo is called without a batch average — a batching bug silently zeros a 5% weight.
commitFreshness=100 for never-released bot repos with auto-commit jobs. No cross-check against contributor count (communityHealth defaults to 50 when flags unset).

No awesome-list pattern detection. Seed-repos.ts currently has 0 awesome-*, but IF one were added (or auto-discovered), nothing in scoring.ts or classification rules specifically discounts readme-dump repos. Issue count (15+ readme lists flagged by OWASP/CheatSheetSeries pattern is at least one in seed) would score full 100 on issueActivity if they had 200+ open issues.
Rank_jump has no guard against ghost climbs — removing a repo from the leaderboard shifts all others up by 1, and the system would flag them with no signal.
fn_patterns: ANY repo with 0 snapshot history (currently 296/309) produces 0 deltas and therefore fires 0 velocity/breakout/quiet-killer signals. The trend engine is structurally BLIND to new repos.

Social-driven breakouts without GitHub stars yet (e.g., a repo trending hard on X before stars catch up): socialBuzzScore=0 everywhere currently, so criterion 3 of breakout never fires. The entire 'social validation' path is dead.

Mega-repo blowups (ollama at 169k stars) never breakout: modifiers.ts:117 gates breakout at stars<1000. Even if ollama went +10k stars tomorrow, breakout multiplier never applies. Only star_velocity_up+star_spike detectors remain, and they have no anti-spam filter.
Quiet-killer requires contributorsDelta30d>0. Currently every repo has 0. Combined with 7d≥30 requirement, 0 firings.
The dampening for 'small repo commit-stale old vanity' kicks at stars>1000 only — stale 500-star repos that get a bot spike are not dampened.

tests_coverage.scoring_engine_tests: ZERO direct tests of computeMomentumScore, scoreRepo, computeScore, computeAllComponents, computeAllModifiers, computeAntiSpamDampening, computeDecayFactor, detectBreakout, detectQuietKiller, classifyMovement, logNorm, linearNorm, freshnessScore, resolveWeights. Grep across all __tests__/*.ts returned no matches.

what_is_tested.reasons.test.ts: detector trigger conditions (release_recent, star_velocity_up, star_spike, rank_jump, breakout_detected pass-through, organic_growth fallback), priority ordering, summary length, formatTimeframe. Tests the detectors with hand-crafted inputs but not the upstream scoring engine that sets isBreakout.
what_is_tested.classification.test.ts: rule-based category classifier (topics/keywords/owner matching). Not scoring.
what_is_tested.filters.test.ts: applyMetaFilter and sort helpers. Tests movementStatus filtering but not how movementStatus is computed.
what_is_tested.featured.test.ts: UI featured-card waterfall logic: rank_jump card mapping, TODAY backfill, no duplicates. Depends on fixtures, not real scoring.
what_is_tested.alerts.test.ts: trigger evaluators (star_spike, new_release, rank_jump, momentum_threshold, breakout_detected transition-in semantics), cooldown window, digest bucketing, rule CRUD validation.
what_is_tested.persistence.test.ts / persistence-hydrat: JSONL IO, atomicity, debouncing, snapshot retention cap.
what_is_tested.scheduler-integration.test.ts: tier assignment (hot/warm/cold), refresh batching, ingestion idempotency.

gap_obvious_from_what_ISNT_tested: NO numeric oracle test: 'given these inputs, computeScore must return X ± 0.1'. Current implementation could silently drift.
NO test for re-normalization bug risk: does resolveWeights preserve sum=1.0 after every override merge?
NO test for cold-start behavior: does a 0-star, 0-delta, null-lastReleaseAt repo produce a sensible score?
NO test for antiSpamDampening thresholds — the fork-delta-vs-total-forks mismatch is unverified.
NO test for breakout criterion interaction: the 2-of-3 rule versus all-3 (legacy scoring.ts) divergence is untested and never caught.
NO test for issueActivity overflow: a repo with 500k open issues silently clamps to 200 ceiling = 100 score, boosting it.
NO test on the MAJOR_VERSION_RE regex for false positives (alpha/beta/rc tags, substring-matched N.0 fragments).

TWO SEPARATE IMPLEMENTATIONS: src/lib/scoring.ts (legacy, used by classifyMovement in that file) and src/lib/pipeline/scoring/engine.ts (new). Drift risk is real — legacy detectBreakout requires absolute delta24≥10 and needs all-3 conditions; new one only needs 2-of-3. No test locks the semantic contract on either.

gaps_and_bugs: BUG: MAJOR_VERSION_RE incorrectly matches pre-release tags like 'langchain-core==1.3.0a3' because the regex uses substring scanning with [^0-9] delimiters and catches '3.0a' as 3.0 followed by 'a'. Already firing in reasons.jsonl.
BUG: antiSpamDampening uses forksDelta7d instead of total forks; code comment at modifiers.ts:67-74 admits it. Miscalibrated threshold 0.005 against a delta instead of a ratio.

BUG: Two forking implementations of the scoring math (src/lib/scoring.ts vs src/lib/pipeline/scoring/engine.ts) diverge on breakout logic, quietKiller logic, and don't share ModifierInput shape. Unclear which is 'the one'.
BUG: categoryMomentum defaults to 0 when scoreRepo is called without categoryAverages — a 5% weight silently zeros in single-repo code paths.

BUG: logNorm ceiling saturation at low ceilings (starVelocity24h cap=500) means any mega-viral spike ≥500/24h is indistinguishable from exactly 500. No way to surface 'extreme' above 'very strong' from a single signal.
GAP: No anti-spam heuristic for small-stars vanity spikes (<200 delta24). Vanity attacks on fresh repos pass through unscathed.
GAP: No stargazer-history backfill observed in .data/snapshots.jsonl — only 13/309 repos have snapshot history. 96% of seed is blind.
GAP: quietKillerBonus is additive (up to +10) AFTER modifiers are applied; this breaks the nice 0-100 contract in edge cases where base*modifiers already = 95 ⇒ 105 clamped to 100.
GAP: No rank-climber guard against ghost climbs (repos shifting because another repo dropped out).
GAP: issueActivity conflates 'healthy project with lots of issues' with 'abandoned project with a backlog'. Should be issue-velocity, not absolute count.
GAP: No tests pin any numeric score outcomes. A regression that multiplies all scores by 2 or shifts the freshness curve would pass the test suite.

GAP: 'Anti-spam dampening' marketing claim (README) is real CODE, but in practice on the current 309-repo dataset fires zero times. It is architecturally present but operationally vapor because (a) zero socialBuzz/zero forksDelta7d makes the gate-1 denominator useless, and (b) the starsDelta24h>200 gate needs history that most repos don't have.
